title: The Pilgrim Way
content:
But once I pass this way,
    And then--no more.
    But once--and then, the Silent Door
    Swings on its hinges,--
    Opens ... closes,--
    And no more
    I pass this way.
    So while I may,
    With all my might,
    I will essay
    Sweet comfort and delight,
    To all I meet upon the Pilgrim Way.
    For no man travels twice
    The Great Highway,
    That climbs through Darkness up to Light,--
    Through Night
    To Day.
meaning:
William Arthur Dunkerley (John Oxenham)'s contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Pilgrim Way"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
